filter_index.video_title: 5 Reasons Why You Should Do The Brahmatal Trek | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: From end of December at base camp; entire landscape white in peak winter (January-February); upper reaches still under snow in spring; snow recedes to higher altitudes (around Brahmatal lake at 12,250 ft) as spring progresses; mostly melted by mid-April
Monsoon makes the trek inaccessible due to rains
temperature_notes: Winter: Day 8-10°C, Night 0 to -8°C; 5 warm layers required
Autumn: Day 10-15°C, Night 0 to -5°C; 4 warm layers required
Spring: Day 15-18°C, Night 0 to 5°C; 3 warm layers required

stated_difficulty: Mostly gradual and steady ascents and descents, except the summit climb on Day 4
experience_suitability: Suitable for fit beginners; first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers are at equal risk of AMS
terrain_challenges: Hard frozen snow in the morning that continues until Brahmatal top, getting steeper near the summit
Rain can flood trails or make them slushy and unsafe
Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut
Summit climb on Day 4 is the main steep/strenuous section; snow section gets steeper approaching the summit
altitude_or_weather_risks: Brahmatal Top at 12,250 ft with gradual elevation gain
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft for any trekker
Acclimatization and adequate water intake are extremely important
Tilandi Campsite (2nd campsite) is known for fast and strong winds; layering is important
Sustained rainfall or snowfall can change trek dynamics, flooding or slushing trails and obstructing paths

fitness_benchmark: To do the Brahmatal Trek comfortably, you must be able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 40 minutes. This is the minimum recommended fitness level that you must aim for.
approval_requirements: Make sure you upload these screenshots 15 to 20 days before your trek date. We want to see that you are being consistent in your preparation.
Your screenshots must demonstrate that you are meeting the fitness criteria for your trek.
prep_window: Follow this, and you'll be trek-ready in 6 weeks.

experience_themes: Winter: snow-covered landscape, Narnia-like vibe from snow-laden oak and rhododendron trees, frozen Bekaltal lake, snow camping at Tilandi with sunrises/sunsets

Autumn: clear post-monsoon mountain views, Trishul range over Ali Bugyal, views of Mt Chaukhamba, Mt Neelkanth, Mt Hathi Ghoda from Jhandi top, vibrant crimson sunsets and golden sunrises at Tilandi, empty trails
Spring: rhododendron (Buransh) blooms in dark pink and crimson, receding snow, rhodo canopy between Bekaltal and Tilandi, blooms near Khorurai

Autumn offers clearest post-monsoon mountain views; Trishul range visible from ridge parallel to Ali Bugyal; Mt Chaukhamba, Mt Neelkanth, Mt Hathi Ghoda visible from Jhandi top; vibrant crimson sunsets and golden sunrises at Tilandi ridge

From end of December at base camp; entire landscape white in peak winter (January-February); upper reaches still under snow in spring; snow recedes to higher altitudes (around Brahmatal lake at 12,250 ft) as spring progresses; mostly melted by mid-April
primary_watchouts: Do not step on frozen Bekaltal lake — ice may not support weight depending on month
Brahmatal lake/top may not be accessible in peak winter due to snow
Winter trek is a notch more difficult due to snow
Summer is not a great season despite access; monsoon makes trek inaccessible
Trekkers seeking rhododendrons in autumn (flowers have wilted)
Trekkers wanting snow in autumn (no snow)
best_for: Trekkers seeking solitude (autumn, after Dusshera/Diwali and before winter)
Photographers of vibrant sunrises/sunsets (autumn at Tilandi)
Rhododendron bloom seekers (spring, especially early March for snow + flowers)
not_ideal_for: Trekkers seeking rhododendrons in autumn (flowers have wilted)
Trekkers wanting snow in autumn (no snow)
Trekkers visiting in summer (not a great season)
Trekkers wanting to reach Brahmatal lake/top in peak winter (may be inaccessible)

needs_review: avoid_months: monsoon months and mid-April are inferred from context, not explicitly named by month
snow_window: end of December phrasing is approximate
video_title: 5 Reasons Why You Should Do The Brahmatal Trek | Indiahikes
